content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration in autonomous agents can be intentionally constrained to match the inherent working memory capacity limits that bound LLM reasoning, creating a self-regulating agent that avoids overloading its working memory by only prioritizing exploration of novel states it can actually hold and reason about. This creates a curious agent that does not waste intrinsic reward on unprocessable, out-of-context novel information that would exceed its working memory reasoning bounds.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
